reference: GATE CS — Probability and Statistics / Probability

map.condition: Need expected value of a discrete RV
RV counts successes in n independent Bernoulli trials
RV counts trials until first success
Continuous RV — need probability over interval

formulas.name: PMF Axioms (Discrete RV)
formulas.plain: P(X=x) >= 0 and sum over all x of P(X=x) = 1
formulas.whenToUse: Verifying or constructing a valid probability mass function for a discrete RV

terms.means: probability that RV X takes value x

formulas.trap: Forgetting to verify that probabilities sum to exactly 1; any constant multiplier missing will make it invalid.

formulas.name: PDF Axioms (Continuous RV)
formulas.plain: f(x) >= 0 and integral from -inf to +inf of f(x) dx = 1
formulas.whenToUse: Verifying or constructing a valid probability density function

terms.means: probability density function of continuous RV X

formulas.trap: PDF values can exceed 1 — only integrals (areas) are probabilities, not f(x) itself.

formulas.name: Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF)
formulas.latex: F(x) = P(X \le x) = \begin{cases} \sum_{t \le x} P(X=t) & \text{discrete} \\ \int_{-\infty}^{x} f(t)\,dt & \text{continuous} \end{cases}
formulas.plain: F(x) = P(X <= x); for discrete: sum of P(X=t) for t<=x; for continuous: integral of f(t) from -inf to x
formulas.whenToUse: Finding probability that X falls below a threshold, or converting between PDF and CDF
formulas.trap: For continuous RVs, P(X = x) = 0; only intervals have positive probability.

formulas.name: PDF from CDF (Continuous RV)
formulas.plain: f(x) = dF(x)/dx
formulas.whenToUse: Recovering the PDF when only the CDF is given
formulas.trap: Differentiating at jump points of a mixed distribution — only valid where F is differentiable.

formulas.name: Expected Value — Discrete
formulas.plain: E[X] = sum over x of x * P(X=x)
formulas.whenToUse: Computing mean (average value) of a discrete RV
formulas.trap: Sum must cover ALL possible values of x; missing tails gives wrong answer.

formulas.name: Expected Value — Continuous
formulas.plain: E[X] = integral of x * f(x) dx from -inf to +inf
formulas.whenToUse: Computing the mean of a continuous RV given its PDF
formulas.trap: Using f(x) as a probability (not an area) when setting up the integral.

formulas.name: Law of the Unconscious Statistician (LOTUS)
formulas.plain: E[g(X)] = sum of g(x)*P(X=x); continuous: integral of g(x)*f(x) dx
formulas.whenToUse: Computing E[X^2], E[1/X], or any function of X without finding the distribution of g(X)

terms.means: any function of the random variable X

formulas.trap: Applying E[g(X)] = g(E[X]) — only true when g is linear; in general E[g(X)] != g(E[X]).

formulas.name: Variance — Computational Formula
formulas.whenToUse: Computing variance — far easier than using the definition E[(X-mu)^2] directly
formulas.trap: Confusing E[X^2] with (E[X])^2 — these are NOT equal (Jensen's inequality).

formulas.name: Standard Deviation
formulas.plain: sigma_X = sqrt(Var(X))
formulas.whenToUse: When the problem asks for spread in the same units as X
formulas.trap: Confusing Var(X) and sigma_X — questions often give one and ask for the other.

formulas.name: Linearity of Expectation
formulas.plain: E[aX + b] = a*E[X] + b
formulas.whenToUse: Scaling or shifting a random variable before computing mean
formulas.trap: Linearity holds even for dependent random variables; Var(aX+b) = a^2 * Var(X) (b drops out).

formulas.name: Variance of Linear Transform
formulas.plain: Var(aX + b) = a^2 * Var(X)
formulas.whenToUse: After scaling/shifting X, finding the new variance

terms.means: shift constant — does not affect variance

formulas.trap: Adding a constant b does NOT change variance (it's a^2, not a^2+b^2).

formulas.name: Bernoulli Distribution
formulas.plain: X ~ Ber(p): P(X=1)=p, P(X=0)=1-p, E[X]=p, Var(X)=p(1-p)
formulas.whenToUse: Single trial with success probability p (coin flip, bit error, etc.)
formulas.trap: Var(X) = p(1-p), not p^2 or p; maximum variance is 1/4 when p=1/2.

formulas.name: Binomial Distribution
formulas.plain: X ~ Bin(n,p): P(X=k) = C(n,k)*p^k*(1-p)^(n-k), E[X]=np, Var(X)=np(1-p)
formulas.whenToUse: Counting successes in n independent, identical Bernoulli trials
formulas.trap: Trials must be independent and p must be the same for each trial; sampling without replacement requires Hypergeometric instead.

formulas.name: Geometric Distribution
formulas.whenToUse: Number of trials until the FIRST success

terms.means: trial on which first success occurs (k >= 1)

formulas.trap: Two conventions exist: 'number of trials' (k>=1) vs 'number of failures before first success' (k>=0); E[X] differs by 1 between them.

formulas.name: Poisson Distribution
formulas.plain: X ~ Poisson(lambda): P(X=k) = e^(-lambda)*lambda^k/k!, k=0,1,2,...; E[X]=Var(X)=lambda
formulas.whenToUse: Counts of rare events in a fixed interval (packets arriving, requests per second)

terms.means: average rate (mean number of events per interval)

formulas.trap: Mean equals variance for Poisson — if mean != variance, it is NOT Poisson.

formulas.name: Uniform Distribution U(a, b)
formulas.whenToUse: RV equally likely to take any value in [a, b]

terms.means: lower and upper bounds of the uniform interval

formulas.trap: Var(X) = (b-a)^2 / 12, NOT (b-a)/12; the factor of 12 is frequently mis-remembered.

formulas.name: Exponential Distribution Exp(lambda)
formulas.plain: X ~ Exp(lambda): f(x)=lambda*e^(-lambda*x) for x>=0; E[X]=1/lambda, Var(X)=1/lambda^2
formulas.whenToUse: Waiting time until next event in a Poisson process; memoryless continuous RV
formulas.trap: Memoryless property: P(X > s+t | X > s) = P(X > t); only exponential distribution has this for continuous RVs.

formulas.name: Normal Distribution N(mu, sigma^2)
formulas.plain: X ~ N(mu, sigma^2): bell curve; E[X]=mu, Var(X)=sigma^2; standard normal Z=(X-mu)/sigma
formulas.whenToUse: Modeling sums of many independent RVs (Central Limit Theorem), IQ scores, measurement errors
formulas.trap: Second parameter in N(mu, sigma^2) is variance, not std deviation; confusing the two leads to Var(X) = (sigma^2)^2 errors.

formulas.name: Independence of Two RVs
formulas.latex: X \perp Y \iff P(X=x, Y=y) = P(X=x)\cdot P(Y=y) \quad \forall\, x, y
formulas.plain: X independent of Y iff P(X=x, Y=y) = P(X=x)*P(Y=y) for all x, y
formulas.whenToUse: Checking whether two RVs are independent (discrete case); continuous: f_{X,Y}(x,y) = f_X(x)*f_Y(y)

terms.means: joint PMF — probability both events occur simultaneously

formulas.trap: Uncorrelated (Cov=0) does NOT imply independent; independence implies uncorrelated but NOT vice versa.

formulas.name: Expectation of Sum (always holds)
formulas.plain: E[X + Y] = E[X] + E[Y]  (holds even if X and Y are dependent)
formulas.whenToUse: Computing mean of a sum of RVs — independence is NOT required

terms.means: any two random variables on the same probability space

formulas.trap: Students assume this requires independence — it does NOT; linearity of expectation is universal.

formulas.name: Variance of Sum (independent RVs)
formulas.plain: If X and Y are independent: Var(X+Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y)
formulas.whenToUse: Computing variance of a sum when independence is established
formulas.trap: Without independence: Var(X+Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y) + 2*Cov(X,Y); omitting the covariance term is the classic error.

formulas.name: Covariance
formulas.plain: Cov(X,Y) = E[XY] - E[X]*E[Y]
formulas.whenToUse: Measuring linear dependence between X and Y; needed for Var(X+Y) when not independent

terms.means: expected value of the product; = E[X]E[Y] iff independent

formulas.trap: Cov(X,X) = Var(X); Cov(aX,bY) = ab*Cov(X,Y).

formulas.name: Markov's Inequality
formulas.plain: P(X >= a) <= E[X] / a  for non-negative X and a > 0
formulas.whenToUse: Upper-bounding tail probabilities when only the mean is known
formulas.trap: X must be non-negative; the bound is loose but requires only knowledge of E[X].

formulas.name: Chebyshev's Inequality
formulas.plain: P(|X - mu| >= k*sigma) <= 1/k^2  for any k > 0
formulas.whenToUse: Bounding probability of deviation from mean when mean and variance are known

terms.means: number of standard deviations away from mean

formulas.trap: Often written as P(|X-mu| >= a) <= Var(X)/a^2; substituting a = k*sigma gives the 1/k^2 form.

formulas.name: Central Limit Theorem (CLT)
formulas.plain: (X_bar - mu) / (sigma/sqrt(n)) -> N(0,1) as n -> inf
formulas.whenToUse: Approximating distribution of sample mean or sum of large n independent identical RVs

terms.means: sample mean of n i.i.d. RVs

formulas.trap: CLT applies to the MEAN (or sum) of i.i.d. RVs, not to individual order statistics or max/min.
